evals.prompt: Create a PRD for adding saved searches with email alerts to this application. Users should be able to save a search, choose how often they want updates, and manage existing alerts from their account area.
evals.expected_output: A GitHub issue draft containing a durable PRD that explores existing search, account, notification, and test patterns before interviewing through assumptions and decision points.
expectations: The workflow launches multiple code-explorer subagents with distinct lenses before writing the PRD.
The workflow performs targeted research from official sources before recording implementation decisions.
The workflow surfaces assumptions early, then asks one user question at a time and includes a recommended answer for each question.

The final PRD follows the required section order (Objective, User Stories, Implementation Decisions, Testing Decisions, Success Criteria, Out of Scope, Further Notes) and includes a GitHub issue title/body plus the intended gh issue create command for offline filing.
Implementation decisions use durable codebase-pattern or official research citations and the PRD avoids specific file paths, code snippets, and brittle command or directory dumps.

evals.prompt: I need a product requirements doc filed as an issue for a team admin audit log. Admins need to see who changed user roles, billing settings, and SSO configuration, with enough detail for compliance review.
evals.expected_output: A PRD issue draft for an admin audit log that investigates existing admin, billing, auth/SSO, compliance, and testing patterns, resolves scope and retention questions, and cites evidence for decisions without brittle implementation detail.
expectations: The workflow gathers codebase context through parallel code-explorer agents and asks each for 5-10 key files with reasons.
The workflow identifies security, retention, permissions, and compliance gaps, surfaces assumptions early, and interviews the user one question at a time.
The workflow researches official security, privacy, platform, or framework guidance before recommending decisions.
The PRD contains an extensive numbered user-story list covering primary, edge, admin, compliance, and error-state scenarios plus concrete success criteria.
Testing decisions focus on external behavior, implementation decisions remain durably cited, and the issue body avoids specific file paths and code snippets.

evals.prompt: Draft and open a PRD issue for improving onboarding: when a new workspace is created, guide the user through inviting teammates, connecting integrations, and completing the first useful setup action.
evals.expected_output: A PRD GitHub issue draft for onboarding improvements that uses codebase exploration, official UX/accessibility research, and a dependency-aware interview before filing.
expectations: The workflow distinguishes product-scope questions from questions answerable by exploration or official documentation.
The workflow walks dependent decisions in order, resolving parent decisions before child decisions and surfacing assumptions before the PRD is written.
The PRD avoids specific file paths, code snippets, and brittle command or project-structure dumps while still citing existing product or architectural patterns.
The Out of Scope section clearly excludes deferred onboarding paths, integrations, or implementation work.
The final output includes the GitHub issue title/body, intended gh issue create command, and any important accepted assumptions or follow-up notes.
